title: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams

seo.imageAlt: A plate of Spaghetti alle Vongole with fresh Manila clams, garlic, parsley, and white wine sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of the Italian coast with this delicious Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams.

description: Experience the true taste of the Italian coast with this classic Spaghetti alle Vongole, featuring fresh Manila clams. This simple yet elegant pasta dish is a celebration of fresh seafood, garlic, white wine, and parsley, creating a light and incredibly flavorful meal perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.notes: like Pinot Grigio or Sauvignon Blanc

instructions.title: Prepare the Clams
instructions.text: Purge the Manila clams by soaking them in a large bowl of cold salted water (about 1 tablespoon of salt per liter) for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours. This helps them release any sand. Drain and rinse thoroughly under cold water. Discard any clams that are open and do not close when gently tapped.

instructions.title: Cook the Spaghetti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente. Before draining, reserve about 1.5 cups of the starchy pasta cooking water. This liquid is essential for creating a silky sauce.

instructions.title: Start the Sauce Base
instructions.text: While the pasta cooks, heat the extra virgin olive oil in a large, deep skillet or pot over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Sauté gently for 2-3 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and lightly golden. Be careful not to burn the garlic, as it will turn bitter.

instructions.title: Steam the Clams
instructions.text: Increase the heat to medium-high. Add the purged clams to the skillet and pour in the dry white wine. Cover the skillet tightly with a lid and cook for 5-7 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until all the clams have opened. Remove the opened clams from the pan and set aside. Discard any clams that remain closed.

instructions.title: Combine Pasta and Sauce
instructions.text: Add the drained spaghetti directly into the skillet with the clam cooking liquid. Add half of the reserved pasta cooking water and a generous amount of fresh chopped parsley. Toss everything together vigorously, allowing the pasta to absorb the flavorful clam broth and create a rich sauce.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Return the opened clams to the skillet. Continue to toss the spaghetti, adding more pasta water as needed, until the sauce emulsifies and beautifully coats the spaghetti. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Garnish with additional fresh parsley before serving immediately for the best flavor.

tags: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams Recipe
tips: Always purge your clams properly. Soaking them in salted water helps remove any grit, ensuring a clean and enjoyable eating experience without sandy surprises.

The pasta cooking water is crucial for creating a silky, emulsified sauce. Its starch content helps bind the clam broth, olive oil, and wine together, making the sauce cling perfectly to the spaghetti.
For an extra layer of richness and depth, you can swirl in a small pat of unsalted butter at the very end when tossing the pasta with the sauce, just before serving. This adds a velvety finish.
article: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams is a beloved Italian Seafood dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
